objective:
A high-level summary (e.g. 2 to 5 sentences) of the main objective(s) and topic(s) of the call described in the grant document should be provided.
 Although this field can be open-ended, a particular interest is placed on whether the investigation of some topics or themes is encouraged within the call, on what is expected to be developped in the project, for which target group(s), and how.
inclusion_criteria:
Any inclusion criteria that applies to the applicants of the call described in the grant document should be listed.
 Descriptions of the inclusion criteria are expected to be relatively brief.
 Information of particular interest includes what type of institutions are allowed to apply, and if any important requirement applies to the applicants.
 If no inclusion criteria can be found, 'None' should be returned.
exclusion_criteria:
Any exclusion criteria that may apply to the applicants of the call described in the grant document should be listed.
 Information of interest for instance includes whether some particular types of institutions are not allowed to apply to the call, or if the participants must not come from specific parts of the world.
 If no exclusion criteria can be found, 'None' should be returned.
deadline:
The deadline for the first stage of the application should be formatted as DD.MM.YYYY.
 As the original grant document may contain several non-deadline dates, specific care should be taken that the correct deadline is identified.
 If several deadlines are present in the document, they should follow the aforementioned format, and be separared by semicolons.
 If no deadline is specified, 'None' should be returned.
max_funding:
The maximum funding amount should be formatted using comma as thousand separators and dot as decimal point, with the associated currency (e.g. euros, dollars).
 If the information is available in the grant document, it should be specified if the amount applies to the whole consortium or to a single partner.
 If no maximum funding amount can be found, 'Not specified' or 'Not specified.' should be returned.
max_duration:
The maximum duration for the project should be returned in months, and formatted as a number followed by 'months'.
 If no maximum duration can be found, 'Not specified' or 'Not specified.' should be returned.
procedure:
Concrete stages of the procedure to submit an application to the grant call should be listed.
 The number of stages (usually 1, 2 or 3) is in particular important.
 A detailed description of each stage is not necessarily expected, but additional practical details regarding the first stage such as language and maximum number of pages allowed for the application, whether the document must be submitted electronically or in written form should be provided.
 If no information about the procedure can be found, 'None' should be returned.
contact:
The contact information should include whenever possible name, e-mail and phone number, in order and separated by commas.
 If several contacts are specified, all contacts should follow the aforementioned format and be separated by semicolons.
 If several contacts share the same contact information (e.g. e-mail, phone number), the information should be repeated for each contact.
 If no contact information can be found, 'None' should be returned.
misc:
Any additional information that can be useful to the applicant can be listed here.
 This field can be open-ended, but information of specific interest includes whether the de-minimis rule applies, what is the Förderquote for different partners, whether specific institutions are expressly invited to apply, whether geographical restrictions apply to the results of the project, or whether an expected starting time for the accpeted projects is provided.
 If no specific additional information can be found, 'None' should be returned.
